pr_title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

comments.author: niole
comments.body:
Would it be useful to a field engineer to see a Jira ticket key in the logs? is this useful information for debugging?

Can we just reference logger?  it should be available

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: It's easier for us to search in new relic for these logs by the jira ticket key and even build alerts on it. If someone else sees this and is curious to open the Jira ticket, they would see we are thinking about removing this code path, so they may even help us identify that this is being used somewhere. I didn't think of that case before, but maybe it would be more useful to make this a WARN log and say something like "This is being deprecated" so if someone that is not us sees this, will definitely try to reach out.

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body:
After some manual tests, I see this code is called for GitHub with PAT for example. Since there is no local clone, the `initiateClone` method does nothing when stale branches are detected. But it's used to invalidate the `fileMetaDataCache` when a branch repoman knows about changed in the remote.
When new branches are added to the remote in this case, there is yet another cache for this that has 60 seconds TTL, so the new branch is picked up when the entry for the list of branches is invalidated `domino.repoman.service.provider.GitHubProvider#fullBranchesQueryCache`.

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: I will remove these logs, since now we know this is being executed.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: This PR introduces drilling of objects through several layers of classes. I assume that this was to make the code more testable, which is great. This can become messy over time if we keep adding objects in this way. We might want to consider adding Guice or some other dependency injection framework at some point

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: Yes, I agree, should we create a Jira for that? I'll mention it in stand up, I don't know if we have some tech debt epic that we actively track, to avoid it getting lost in backlog.

comments.author: niole
comments.body:
here is the develop tech debt epic: https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-49138?jql=text%20~%20%22develop%20tech%20debt*%22

It seems more like a graveyard for tech debt tickets. tbh, repoman is pretty stable regarding the classes that exist inside of it. we aren't really adding much. maybe we should just keep guice in mind, but not file a ticket. it might not be super necessary

pr_title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

comments.author: niole
comments.body: Why shouldn't this setting be here?

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body:
The GitProvider class currently has logic to interact with local clones, but it doesn't know when a local clone needs to be invalidated/replaced/refreshed, that is managed by RepoManagerActor. So this config should be there. But the problem is that the `staleBranches` method is also implemented for GitHub and GitLab APIs, when not using local clones. But repoman only uses this method in the context of local clones. So it's unclear why we have those implementations for GitHub/GitLab APIs (https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-55245).

For now the safest/easiest place to put this config was here, without affecting anything else. Ideally the `staleBranches` method should not take the `branches` parameter (which here is just a "filter by this branches"). So you call this and this gives you all branches new/modified in the remote. Then repoman based on the setting can decide if it uses all of them or filters them by the `branches` it knows about. This approach cannot be implemented unless we remove the code for GitHub/GitLab APIs that relies on this `branches` parameter, which makes no sense to me.

pr_title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

comments.author: niole
comments.body: Why did you change this to be a class?

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: To be able to mock it where it was used. It's like all methods defined were all static before, and with this change they are instance methods now. So they can be mocked easily by passing a mock instance of this to the objects that use it.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: got it

pr_title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

comments.author: niole
comments.body: do you mean this?: "return new branches in the remote when configured to refresh local clone if new branches exist"

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: yes, thanks!

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Key change to decrease heartbeat frequency and therefore system load. Open to leaving this as is or using a different value here instead

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: Increasing to 2 minutes seems fine to me, I tracked down [the original commit configuring it to 60 seconds](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/commit/3631b4307410ab1b9caecd1daf506289e69c61de#diff-14f9f485c6362fefa0de89592f5b4b91fa366d6874af52d37ce1db5660ef03e5R20-R23) and there doesn't seem to be any particular reason that 60 seconds was chosen/is important.

comments.author: ddl-ebrown
comments.body: I presume the downside is the reporting status in the UI is less frequent when something errors?

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Yeah I think that is probably the most relevant case where this could be painful, as most happy-path cases shouldn't need heartbeats to transition correctly. Even a normal run failure is managed by the executor which should be able to trigger a transition to a terminal state, but there are probably some failure modes where messages don't/can't send and heartbeats are our only recourse

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Triggers heartbeat generation to stop for this saga

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body:
Instead of a class that generates heartbeats, we have one that makes sure actors for active sagas exist when after a nucleus restart. Once actors exist they can handle creating the heartbeats.

Note: This can lead to a flood of heartbeats when if the dispatcher were to restart when there were a lot of sagas, but in the long run the cadence of heartbeats should smooth out as new sagas are created and existing sagas complete. I think this potential spike at startup is probably ok.

comments.author: ddl-ebrown
comments.body: Is there any way to skew the distribution here so that we don't have a thundering herd?

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: I considered just adding a sleep between each publish, happy to add that if you think it's valuable.

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Not necessary because the `@Inject` annotation for these classes is already capable creating these classes. This would be necessary if we wanted to bind an instance of a trait to an implementation of the trait or if we wanted to do some logic to unpack existing classes into something more digestible by the constructors for these classes.

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Oh yeah I had renamed the class but didn't rename these, let me do that

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: It could be nice to let the `1000` here be configurable (CC value?) – if this change turns out to cause problems, theoretically setting the value from `1000` to `0` via config would "revert" the change if I understand correctly, which I think would be easier than doing a full revert.

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: On the other hand, it could be a hassle/not a benefit to make it configurable – I'm okay with this as-is.

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: I'm ok with it as is, at this point we are dealing with an edge case within an edge case as this class is only relevant for nucleus restarts if there are tons of sagas and even without a delay we'd get behavior on par or better than what we have now

pr_title: [DOM-54487] - Decouple ModelApiPredictionDatasetCreator from monolith

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body:
I'm not sure I followed this correctly -- do we already have an "internal" model monitoring API in nucleus that DMM calls?
If so, does this function need to part of a public API, or could it be internal only for callbacks from model serving?

comments.author: ddl-tnguyen
comments.body:
Yeah I agree it's a bit confusing. The "internal" API (implemented at [ModelMonitoringApiImpl](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/02c085d6f9d9411af0e4514397d4268cf8d6fa6c/model-monitoring/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/modelmonitoring/implementation/ModelMonitoringApiImpl.scala#L18)) is actually for nucleus to make calls to DMM Plier. If you look at the implementation, we're actually just making HTTP calls to plier. The yamls for that API live in `dmm-api-backend` and we just use the `domino-play-server` template as a way of bringing those types into nucleus as scala code. 

We don't expose that API anywhere and it can't be hit by an external service, so that's why I created the separate public API.

comments.author: ddl-tnguyen
